name: John ibn Huwai
title: Servant of Abu Dharr
shortDescription: The loyal black servant of Abu Dharr al-Ghifari who sacrificed the most colorful blood for the love of the Ahl al-Bayt in Karbala.
fullStory:
John ibn Huwai was a black servant from Nubia (Africa) who served the great companion of the Prophet, Abu Dharr al-Ghifari. After Abu Dharr's passing, he entered the service of the Ahl al-Bayt (AS) and lived alongside Amir al-Mu'minin, Imam Hasan, and Imam Hussain (AS) with complete loyalty for years. He was skilled in repairing weapons and was busy preparing the swords alongside Imam Hussain (AS) on the night of Ashura.

On the day of Ashura, when it was the companions' turn to sacrifice themselves, John came to Imam Hussain (AS) asking for permission to fight. The Imam, not wanting this devoted elder to be killed, kindly said to him: 'O John, you accompanied us for peace and comfort, so you are now free and can leave'. These words broke John's heart, and weeping, he threw himself at the Imam's feet.

According to authentic historical accounts, John tearfully pleaded: 'O son of the Prophet, is it fair that I eat from your table during days of ease, but abandon you in times of hardship? It is true that my scent is bad, my lineage is lowly, and my color is black, but breathe upon me a heavenly breath so my scent becomes pleasant, my lineage noble, and my face white. By God, I will not part from you until my black blood mixes with your pure blood'.

Seeing this depth of understanding and love, Imam Hussain (AS) granted him permission to fight. Despite his old age, John entered the battlefield with immense bravery, reciting an epic poem and fighting until he fell to the ground under multiple blows from the enemy and attained martyrdom.

After his martyrdom, Imam Hussain (AS) rushed to the lifeless body of this black servant. The Imam placed his holy face upon John's blood-stained face and made an eternal prayer for him: 'O God, whiten his face, sweeten his scent, and resurrect him with the righteous'. It is said that after this prayer, John's body emitted the scent of musk, becoming an eternal symbol of equality and pure love in the Hussaini school.
quoteArabic: وَاللّهِ لا أُفارِقُکُمْ حَتّی یَخْتَلِطَ هذَا الدَّمُ الأسْوَدُ مَعَ دِمائِکُم
quote: By God, I will not part from you until my black blood mixes with your pure blood.
